data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: CLEAN — no findings.

T8 APPLIES: this epic's plan text defines/references LLM-review-system constructs (the criteria registry with `exec` tiers, the ISF "intent-source fidelity" criterion + its 2-step protocol, the agent-vs-single-turn bright line, the three-pass evidence/verify/gate model, and the Pass-4 MOVE REGISTRY referenced in the seed concerns). I tool-grounded each referenced enum/schema/protocol/artifact and probed for the five structural-gap classes T8 hunts. No proven gap; the constructs this epic names are fully specified in already-shipped sibling machinery (epics 5fd2/9da1/e6d9, all closed) and resolve to real source.

Structural-gap checklist (all PASS):
- (a) No referenced enum whose value vocabulary is undefined. The criterion exec-tier vocabulary `DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT` is fully enumerated and closed in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:31` (merged-descriptor `"exec"`) and used consistently in the plan's bright-line prose. The ISF verdict/three-pass `{yes|no|insufficient}` + severity-attribute vocab are defined in 5fd2/9da1 and the shipped passes, not left open here.
- (b) The ISF processing protocol/decision rule is co-located with the schema/context it needs: the plan states ISF fires ONLY when the ticket is LINKED TO A SESSION LOG, runs on a frontier model, and is a 2-STEP "extract expressed requirements → check the plan-graph against each" comparison over artifacts already held (session log + pre-resolved ticket graph) — firing condition, tier, and protocol are stated together. The criterion itself is shipped + validated under closed story 681b (registry.py ISF, prompt files plan_review_ISF.md/isf_finder.md/isf_summarizer.md, eval `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml`).
- (c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. This is a remediation/doc/eval epic (WS-DOC, WS-EVAL-EXISTING, WS-VISUAL-EVAL remaining); it introduces no new LLM-decision counter/state of its own — the finding-`id` mint + advisory cap remain owned by the orchestrator (ca03) in 5fd2.
- (d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified. The ISF criterion specifies the context-overflow fallback explicitly (a DETERMINISTIC pre-retrieval step — chunk/grep decision-bearing sections + pre-fetch the ticket graph — "never an autonomous agent loop"); the no-linked-intent-source case is a specified not-applicable/skip ("never fabricate an intent baseline"); the gate's fail-open/INDETERMINATE postures are inherited from the shipped three-pass design.
- (e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern. The epic AFFIRMATIVELY documents this very concern — the "Anti-false-positive framing must be PINK-ELEPHANT-SAFE (affirmative)" bullet and the prompt-engineering-guidance bullet prescribe affirmative framing over negation and atomic checklists co-located with each criterion — so the antipattern is handled by design, not present. Referenced agents/skills/enums all exist (tool-verified): the criteria registry + exec-tier vocab, the ISF eval spec, and the three-pass passes resolve to real source.

Affirmations (T8): every referenced LLM-system enum/protocol/schema/artifact this epic names exists and is fully specified (tool-verified); the ISF decision rule is co-located with its firing condition + 2-step protocol + overflow fallback; no undefined-but-referenced vocabulary, no ambiguous counter, no unspecified sub-step fallback, and no instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern (the epic prescribes the affirmative substitute). No T8 findings to action.
